published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML: standard AIPW/DML Neyman-orthogonal score identity under unconfoundedness

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform: DML/LRO/ADML studentization frontier is adjacent but not the Foster-Syrgkanis excess-risk-to-band non-implication

published_prior_art.cite: FosterSyrgkanis2023OSL + SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE: papers are orthogonal but neither publishes the shortcut being refuted
ChernozhukovChetverikovKato2017HDCLT / ChernozhukovChetverikovKato2017EmpiricalBootstrap: centered Gaussian/bootstrap maxima tools, not this drift calculation
KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE + ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE: adjacent smoothness/necessity results, not this open-class band-undercoverage witness

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The negative kernel targets an excess-risk-to-Gaussian-band shortcut, but no cited paper or named workflow is shown to use Foster-Syrgkanis excess-risk guarantees as sufficient for Semenova-Chernozhukov simultaneous HTE bands; tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The open-class witness cites KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE and ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE by bibkey only, without a section/equation/page anchor tying them to the specific simultaneous-band gap.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The drift b_{n,j} uses undefined P_{\widehat\eta} and does not condition on cross-fit training randomness; the feasible-process empirical-process remainder beyond mean drift is not represented in Lambda_n or G_n.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed loss lower bound Phi(Lambda_n/2)-1/2 does not follow from the Gaussian max-shift calculation; coverage loss depends on alpha and the max quantile, and can be smaller for high-coverage bands.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Theorem 2 with the exact finite-M or Gumbel-limit alpha-dependent max-shift coverage formula, explicitly defining one-sided versus two-sided bands and the calibrated quantile.

Retarget Conjecture 1 against a specific published or software inference workflow that actually treats orthogonal risk/excess-risk as enough for simultaneous HTE bands, or downgrade the claim to a diagnostic for Semenova-Chernozhukov high-level assumptions.
Redefine b_{n,j} conditionally on training samples and add an explicit stochastic equicontinuity/remainder term R_n to the frontier so Lambda_n is not missing the feasible-process error.
Add location-level anchors for every flagship comparator, especially Kennedy et al. 2024 and Armstrong-Kolesar 2021 in Conjecture 2.

recommended_next_step: Do not send this version to Stage 0; either pivot or rewrite the kernel as a named-workflow necessity result with a correct alpha-dependent coverage calculation.
